text.title: Guardrails Implementation Failure Modes
text.summary: Five recurring failure modes in production guardrails implementations.

antipattern.description: Hardcoded thresholds and rule constants in pipeline code.
antipattern.symptom: Rule tweaks require code review + deploy + reroll; rules change less often than they should.
antipattern.root-cause: Config dataclass not used; constants live inline.
antipattern.fix: Single GuardrailConfig dataclass loaded from YAML/JSON (rule r1).

antipattern.symptom: Validator outage releases unfiltered traffic; only discovered via incident.
antipattern.root-cause: try/except Exception: pass to "keep service up".
antipattern.fix: Fail loud + log + block per fail_closed (rule r4).

antipattern.symptom: Cannot tell from logs whether a block occurred or just a transform; audit unclear.
antipattern.fix: Strict separation: validators return ok/blocked; filters return text only (rule r3).

antipattern.description: Synchronous serial fan-out under high traffic.
antipattern.symptom: p95 latency exceeds budget; tail spikes during high QPS.
antipattern.root-cause: Pipeline runs checks serially even though they are independent.
antipattern.fix: ThreadPoolExecutor / asyncio fan-out for independent checks (rule r5).

antipattern.description: No structured audit log — violations land only in unstructured stdout.
antipattern.symptom: Cannot run "top blocked rules by tenant" query during postmortem.
antipattern.fix: Structured violation log with rule_id + tenant + feature + input_hash (rule r6).
